summary: Backend-only verification completed for plugin licensing integration using static code review plus pytest execution. Runtime API validation against public URL could not run because NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and MOREPHI_PUBLIC_CLIENT_TOKEN were not configured; all pytest cases were skipped. The claimed JWT_SECRET-derived signing fallback was asserted against /tmp/more-phi-landing-page/backend/server.py, which is outside the repository and could not be verified against store-backend/src/lib/crypto.ts (current repo requires LICENSE_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY and has no JWT fallback). Success rates split into tracks; skipped tests score 0.0.

backend_issues.notes: Prior critical claim about JWT_SECRET-derived signing fallback could not be reproduced in repository source. Current store-backend/src/lib/crypto.ts and store-backend/src/config/env.ts require LICENSE_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY and provide only an explicit dev-key bypass.

minor.issue: Payload includes product_id but endpoint does not validate product_id against activation/license context in current store-backend/src/routes/v1/plugin-licenses.routes.ts.

Coverage currently only checks missing token and unknown license paths; no runtime assertion for successful activate/refresh response schema (certificate_payload/signature/public_key_id) or deactivation success path.

All plugin/purchasing pytest cases were skipped due to missing NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and/or MOREPHI_PUBLIC_CLIENT_TOKEN in this container context. Skipped tests are not counted as passed.
Claims about /tmp/more-phi-landing-page/backend/server.py cannot be verified against the repository and are excluded from verified findings.

action_items: Provide public runtime env values (NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL or REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, and MOREPHI_PUBLIC_CLIENT_TOKEN) and rerun plugin API tests against live preview endpoint.
Add plugin API positive-path tests covering activate, refresh, and deactivate response/body validation in the repository test suite.
Run full CMake + Catch2 test suite in an environment with cmake/JUCE toolchain.
critical_code_review_comments: Plugin signing key lifecycle: current repository requires explicit LICENSE_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY and has no JWT-derived fallback. If a separate /tmp backend exists, ensure it is synchronized with repository policy.
C++ compatibility mapping looks correct: ActivationClient endpoints/path updates and certificate field parsing align with backend response shape; MPHI and MPH1 parsing coexist as intended.

success_rate.methodology: Weighted sum per track: 35% static code review, 15% local syntax/compile checks, 25% unit tests, 25% integration tests. Skipped tests score 0.0. Scores are split into Python backend and C++/JUCE plugin tracks.

python_backend.notes: Static review and Python lint/compile checks completed. All pytest cases skipped (0/18 executed across full + plugin-license reports); no runtime API validation. Prior 0.4 unit-test score was undocumented and has been corrected to 0.0.

cpp_plugin.notes: Static review only. CMake missing; no compile or runtime verification performed.

test_credentials: Read: /app/memory/test_credentials.md (no private creds provided for this integration).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest ran successfully but skipped runtime API cases because BASE_URL/token envs were missing. Focus next run on live endpoint validation of plugin activate/refresh/deactivate including certificate payload/signature/public_key_id contract and C++ verifier compatibility. Verify against repository paths (store-backend/), not /tmp/. Skipped tests must not be counted as passed.
rca of the issue: Primary blocker RCA: runtime tests depend on env-injected public URL and plugin token; these were not present, so pytest fixtures intentionally skipped cases. Prior signing-key claim could not be verified against repository source and has been removed from verified findings.
